title: Author tests for cross-team data visibility and injection vulnerabilities in ProjHub API

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects (list endpoint) returns only projects belonging to the caller's team, not projects from other teams.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The /projects endpoint lacks team-scoped filtering, returning all projects regardless of caller's team. This is the primary IDOR vector (CWE-639) enabling cross-team data enumeration.

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id} returns a 403 (or 404) for a project belonging to a different team than the caller's.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Direct object reference by ID bypasses any list-level filtering. Without ownership validation on detail retrieval, an attacker with a valid project ID can read another team's data (CWE-284).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id}/tasks returns a 403 (or 404) for a project belonging to a different team, preventing cross-team task listing.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Tasks inherit project-level visibility boundaries. Without parent-project ownership check on task listing, cross-team task enumeration is possible (CWE-639).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id} returns a 403 (or 404) for a task belonging to a cross-team project.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The task detail endpoint performs no parent-project ownership check, enabling transitive IDOR through task IDs (CWE-639).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /tasks/{id} returns a 403 for a task in a cross-team project, preventing unauthorized modification.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Without team-scoped write guards, any authenticated user can modify tasks in other teams' projects. This is a cross-team data tampering vector (CWE-284).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin DELETE /tasks/{id} returns a 403 for a task in a cross-team project, preventing unauthorized deletion.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Without team-scoped delete guards, any authenticated user can delete tasks in other teams' projects. This is a cross-team data destruction vector (CWE-284).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id} response (for an own-team task) does not contain admin-only fields: internal_priority, reviewer_notes, security_classification, estimated_cost.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The full task model including privileged fields is serialized to all callers. These fields contain cost estimates and security labels that violate least-privilege disclosure (CWE-200).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id}/comments filters out comments marked internal: true, only returning public comments.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Internal comments may contain security notes, reviewer feedback, or sensitive discussion. All comments are returned unfiltered, leaking internal communications to non-admin users (CWE-200).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that non-admin project export, share, or duplicate responses do not leak admin-only fields (internal_metrics, admin_config, budget_allocation).
ground_truth_issues.justification: Export/share/duplicate endpoints produce full project snapshots. All fields including admin-only data are copied, creating secondary leakage channels that bypass detail-endpoint redaction (CWE-200).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /admin/audit-log returns a 403, preventing non-admin access to the system audit log.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The audit log endpoint uses require_auth instead of require_admin, allowing any authenticated user to read all system operations including user IDs and sensitive actions (CWE-862).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /admin/reports/summary returns a 403, preventing non-admin access to org-wide metrics and budget data.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The summary report endpoint uses require_auth instead of require_admin, leaking cross-team metrics and total budget allocation to any authenticated user (CWE-862).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that the GET /search/tasks endpoint is not vulnerable to SQL injection — a UNION SELECT or similar injection payload in the q parameter must not return data from other tables (e.g. internal_priority, reviewer_notes from task_index) or cause the endpoint to leak sensitive columns.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The search query is constructed via string concatenation without parameterization. A UNION-based injection extracts privileged columns from task_index, bypassing all application-layer access controls (CWE-89).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/generate-report does not allow OS command injection via the title or format parameter — shell metacharacters (e.g. semicolons, backticks, pipes) must not execute arbitrary commands.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The report generation endpoint passes user-supplied title directly to a shell command via subprocess with shell=True. An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands (CWE-78).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that GET /debug/users is either not accessible or does not return plaintext API tokens/credentials. The endpoint must require admin authentication or be removed entirely.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The /debug/users endpoint dumps all user records including plaintext API tokens. This enables full account takeover of any user without requiring their credentials (CWE-200).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that GET /debug/config and GET /debug/env are either not accessible without admin authentication or are removed, preventing exposure of internal system configuration and environment variables.
ground_truth_issues.justification: These debug endpoints expose database connection strings, secret keys, and runtime environment variables. Accessible without authentication, they provide an attacker with infrastructure-level intelligence (CWE-200, CWE-1295).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that deprecated API endpoints (GET /api/v1/tasks, GET /api/v1/projects) are either removed, require authentication, or return 404/403 — they must not serve data without authentication.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Legacy v1 API endpoints have no authentication and return all data including admin-only fields. They are an improper assets management vulnerability allowing unauthenticated full data access (CWE-1295).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /tasks/{id} cannot overwrite admin-only fields (internal_priority, security_classification, estimated_cost) — mass assignment of privileged fields must be rejected or ignored.
ground_truth_issues.justification: PATCH payloads are applied directly to the task model without field-level allowlisting. A non-admin can escalate data by writing to privileged fields like security_classification (CWE-915).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /projects/{id} cannot overwrite admin-only fields (internal_metrics, admin_config, budget_allocation) — mass assignment of privileged fields must be rejected or ignored.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Same mass-assignment flaw as RUB-017 but on the project model. Unguarded PATCH allows non-admins to tamper with budget_allocation and admin_config, corrupting project governance data (CWE-915).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that POST /tasks/{id}/notify-webhook does not allow SSRF — the webhook_url parameter must be validated to prevent requests to internal/private IP addresses (127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, 10.x, etc.).
ground_truth_issues.justification: The webhook_url is fetched server-side without validation, allowing SSRF attacks against internal services and cloud metadata endpoints (CWE-918).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that error responses from the application do not include full Python stack traces, internal file paths, or framework version information.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The application returns verbose tracebacks with file paths and dependency versions in error responses. This information aids attackers in crafting targeted exploits against known library vulnerabilities (CWE-209).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/share-link does not use a predictable or reversible token generation algorithm (e.g. MD5 of the project ID) — share tokens must be cryptographically random.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The share-link endpoint derives the token from MD5 of the project ID, making all share tokens predictable if the project ID is known (CWE-328).

ground_truth_issues.description: An anti-overblock test asserts that an admin GET /projects (list endpoint) STILL returns all projects from all teams with full detail including internal_metrics, admin_config, budget_allocation.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Security fixes must not break admin visibility. This guard ensures the model's patch correctly scopes restrictions to non-admin users and does not accidentally strip admin data.

ground_truth_issues.description: An anti-overblock test asserts that a same-team non-admin participant CAN still access their own team's projects and tasks (returns 200, not 403).
ground_truth_issues.justification: Security fixes must not break legitimate access. This guard ensures the model's patch correctly scopes restrictions to cross-team requests and does not accidentally deny same-team users their own data.

ground_truth_issues.description: Anti-overblock tests assert that a non-admin participant CAN still create tasks, add comments, and upload attachments in their own team's projects (returns 200, not 403).
ground_truth_issues.justification: Create operations in own-team projects are legitimate user actions. Over-restrictive fixes that block these break core usability.

ground_truth_issues.description: An anti-overblock test asserts that a non-admin participant CAN still call /projects/{id}/duplicate, /projects/{id}/share, and /projects/{id}/export on their own team's projects (returns 200, not 403).
ground_truth_issues.justification: These action endpoints are legitimate team-member functionality. Over-restrictive fixes that block own-team duplicate/share/export degrade usability and indicate imprecise access-control scoping.

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin cannot use path traversal (e.g., .. segments) in /tasks/{id}/attachments/{path} to reach files outside the task's attachment directory.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The attachment download endpoint uses os.path.join with user-supplied filename without validating against path traversal, allowing access to arbitrary files on the server (CWE-22).

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that GET /debug/dump is either not accessible without admin authentication or is removed, preventing unauthenticated full database dump exposure.
ground_truth_issues.justification: The /debug/dump endpoint exports the entire database contents without authentication. This is the highest-severity data exposure vector, warranting explicit coverage (CWE-200).

ground_truth_issues.description: Tests seed sentinel marker bytes into admin-only fields (reviewer_notes, internal_metrics, admin_config) and assert those marker bytes do not appear anywhere in non-admin responses. Strictly stronger than status-code-only assertions.
ground_truth_issues.justification: Status-code checks alone cannot detect partial data leakage in 200-OK responses. Sentinel markers prove data-level isolation by verifying privileged content is absent from the response body, not just the status line.
